title: Fix category page infinite scroll by extracting useCategoryProductsInfinite hook

requirements.summary: Create a new custom hook `useCategoryProductsInfinite` that encapsulates all pagination logic for category products, managing accumulated products, page state, loading states, and error handling internally
acceptanceCriteria: Hook returns `{ products, isLoading, isLoadingMore, error, hasMore, loadMore, totalCount }`
Calling `loadMore` appends next page products to the accumulated array without clearing existing ones
Changing `categoryId` resets accumulated products and page to initial state
Duplicate fetches are prevented when a fetch is already in flight
Hook cleans up timers/state on unmount

file_operations.description: Create a custom hook that mirrors the architectural pattern of `useHomepageCategoriesInfinite`. The hook must: accept `categoryId` (string) and `pageSize` (number) parameters; internally manage an accumulated products array, current page number (starting at 1), initial loading state, load-more loading state, hasMore flag, and totalCount; use `useCategoryProductsPaginated` as the underlying data fetcher; implement an in-flight guard using a ref to prevent duplicate concurrent fetches; expose `{ products, isLoading, isLoadingMore, error, hasMore, loadMore, totalCount }` return interface; reset all internal state (products array, page, flags) when `categoryId` or `pageSize` changes using a useEffect dependency; clean up any pending state or refs on unmount. The `loadMore` callback must check the in-flight guard, increment the page, fetch the next page via the paginated hook with `refetch()`, and append results to the accumulated array. Include proper error handling and ensure `hasMore` is false when accumulated products count equals totalCount.

requirements.summary: Refactor CategoryPage to use the new `useCategoryProductsInfinite` hook, removing all manual pagination state management and timer-based logic while preserving Spanish UI text
acceptanceCriteria: CategoryPage no longer contains `accumulatedProducts` state, manual `page` state, or `useEffect`-based accumulation logic
Initial load shows a centered spinner; product grid renders once products are available
Scrolling to the bottom triggers `loadMore` and shows a spinner below the existing product grid without hiding it
Previously loaded products remain fully visible during a paginated fetch
New products append below existing products without any flicker or grid reset
Spinner disappears after the paginated fetch completes
End-of-results message appears in Spanish when all products are loaded
Desktop loads 10 products per page; mobile loads 5 products per page

file_operations.description: Refactor the component to use the new `useCategoryProductsInfinite` hook and remove all manual pagination state management. Remove: `page` state, `accumulatedProducts` state, and any `useEffect` hooks that manually accumulate products or manage timers. Replace with: a single call to `useCategoryProductsInfinite(categoryId, pageSize)` where `pageSize` is determined by `useIsMobile()` (5 for mobile, 10 for desktop). Use `useInfiniteScroll` with a throttled scroll handler (500px threshold from bottom) to trigger the `loadMore` callback. Add a sentinel ref using `useIntersectionObserver` as a secondary trigger. Render structure: (1) Initial full-screen centered spinner shown only when `isLoading` is true and products array is empty; (2) ProductGrid rendered whenever `products.length > 0`, always visible during pagination; (3) A separate loading spinner div rendered below the grid when `isLoadingMore` is true; (4) An end-of-results message in Spanish ('No hay más productos en esta categoría') when `hasMore` is false and `products.length > 0`. Preserve all existing Spanish UI text including the category heading and breadcrumb navigation. Do not modify any global styles or `index.css`. Ensure the product grid never unmounts or resets during pagination, maintaining scroll position and visual continuity.
